dccb.title: [DMR-CCB] max turbo frequency (MTF) as defined by p0n/2 is insufficient for competitiveness, p0n/4 is required for DMR-AP/SP
dccb.description: it is proposed to redefine  max turbo freq. down to bucket 0 min 25% cc target (p0n/4) in the goal of reaching >+2 bins vs. p0n/2.   Justification:  MSFT/GO/AWS have internal KPI on low cc subcomponent vs. prior gen. POR limits  freq  to p0n/2. GO  sku  +2b redefinition cost $12.2M. The POR turbo schedule methodology limits max turbo  freq  to 50% of active cores and DMR is trending toward lower frequency than GNR. This CCB seeks to support a lower utilization scenario turbo schedule vs. GNR to and address the following: Support asks from MSFT,&n

dccb.title: Making DTS Temperature register codes available to DDR IP
dccb.description: With the higher speed of DDR interface in DMR, the existing method of temperature compensation is proving to be inadequate for DDR link timing. DDR IP is moving to a DTS based compensation scheme for the sensitive analog circuit. DDR IP will host 3 remote sensor diodes which will be connected to DTS, residing in SOC. The Temperature readings of three diodes are stored in DTS registers and sent to Punit through PMSB. dccb.title: [DMR-Power] PCIe ,CXL and UXI L0p support
dccb.description: HSD tracks support dynamic PCIe, CXL and UXI L0P support. Based on PCIe specs L0P support is required for x8, x4, x2 and x1.   L0P support is planned in 2 modes. (1) Autonomous mode In Autonomous mode symmetric L0P entry and exit is managed by controller automatically. Controller will enter x8 L0P on its own as PCIe/CXL traffic drops(leaky bucket algorithm) . Controller will continue to enter deeper L0P states(x8 -> x4 -> x2 -> x1) as bandwidth continues to drop. Controller will provide configuration controls to enable/disable L0Pbifurcations. It should also provide L0P entry/exi

dccb.title: Simplify RAPL Interfaces and interactions in PrimeCode
dccb.description: In GNR there are four different interfaces which control the RAPL limits: 1.  MSR Interface:  This is non architectural MSR used by driver, OS or inband S/W . This is used to adjust RAPL constraints below the limits set by BIOS/CSR. 2.  TPMI Interface:  This is MMIO interface which can be accessed by drivers and OS alike. dccb.title: [DMR-CCB] DMR Power-On Configs (Single DIMM Power-On Config)
dccb.description: This HSD is to track the changes needed for a non-POR power-on configuration with a single DIMM. Ideally, this DIMM can be populated on any channel. To support a single DIMM, we’ll need to route all requests to a single IMH quadrant. From the CBB’s perspective, the simplest thing I can think of is to disable 3 out of the 4 columns of CBOs so that requests naturally target the right CBB-IMH link. At a minimum, this needs support in the CBB as well as disabling of IOCA in one half of the IMH to route requests to the pair of HAMVF which have the single DIMM populated.

dccb.description: GNR Si root caused a PC6 entry x ITD bug. The RC FSM is setup in such a fashion its Qchannel FSM and DVFS FSM is independent. Before the RC enters its Idle state there is a timer that needs to expire. While the MDFI, fabric and other IPs have been already queisced  and RC is in the processing of entering Idle state. If an ITD request comes along it leads to a hang waiting for the MDFI to ack the voltage change request. dccb.title: [DMR-CCB] Open iMH-CBB d2d bridge for all traffic before Pcode requests timestamp
dccb.description: Today d2d bridge is opened only after TSC is complete with CBB Pcode.  Until then d2d bridge will allow messages with PM_DIE_2_DIE_SAI only.  Other messages are filtered.  However Pcode does now use PM_DIE_2_DIE_SAI when it requests timestamp.  Thus its message will be filtered and S3M will keep waiting for TSC request, thus causing hang. Multiple ways to solve the problem.  Submitting this HSD to track the arch fix.
dccb.full_text: [dmr-ccb] open imh-cbb d2d bridge for all traffic before pcode requests timestamp today d2d bridge is opened only after tsc is complete with cbb pcode.  until then d2d bridge will allow messages with pm_die_2_die_sai only.  other messages are filtered.  however pcode does now use pm_die_2_die_sai when it requests timestamp.  thus its message will be filtered and s3m will keep waiting for tsc request, thus causing hang. multiple ways to solve the problem.  submitting this hsd to track the arch fix.

dccb.title: Punit to remove support for Socket Rapl Extensions
dccb.description: Originally socket RAPL extensions were limited in scope. Since then converged telemetry has come about and has architecturally replaced socket RAPL extensions with a more general architecture. GNR generation does not use socket RAPL extensions. In fact, Primecode has repurposed the memory range used for socket RAPL extensions for other purposes and Primecode does not even support socket RAPL extensions. This hsd is filed to formalize PUnit removal of support for socket RAPL extensions. If this is approved PUnit will: 1. remove the MMIO range for socket RAPL access to PUnit. 2. deprecate the so

dccb.title: [DMR][PrimeCode] ISA signals handling MSM Aggregator
dccb.description: At high level here is the oobmsm architecture in DMR, where each IMH (root and leaf) has its own OOBMSM and MSM Aggregator(an small version of OOBMSM), as you can see, two instances by IMH dielet, each instance runs it own oCode FW, hence config req/ack/done is required per OOBMSM or MSM instance, separately.   There is one isa_oob per IMH dialet, and each isa_oob is a [31:0] bit vector, so the requirement for isa signals configuration might be like this: IMH _root .isa_oob->config_req/ack/done[ 0 ] for IMH _root .oobmsm IMH _root .isa_oob->config_req/ack/done[ 1 ] for IMH _root .ms

dccb.title: [DMR-CCB] Implementation Simplification in setting the CXL.io field in a PCIe Translation Completion TLP rooted from a Type 1/2 CXL Device
dccb.description: Feature Description • Employs an optimized method to identify DMA that must be placed over the CXL.io link by a Type 1/2 CXL device, aka setting of the CXL.io field in a PCIe Translation Completion TLP rooted from a Type 1/2 CXL device o The optimized method sets to 1 the CXL.io field when DMA targets Non-Coherent regions. o The Non-Coherent regions are identified based on TOLM, TOHM and some fixed address range below 1MB address o Background: the existing method uses Memory Type Range Registers (MTRR) and Page Attribute Table (PAT) based algorithm . dccb.title: ULA needs global ART value over serial download wire from Punit
dccb.description: In GNR, ULA had an integrated DVP (which is a recipient of global timestamp via ADL from Punit), thus providing access to the global timestamp.  In DMR, DVP is outside the ULA, and ULA needs time stamp for correlating its flit-logger with DVP.  Hence, ULA needs to implement a time sync serial download interface, in order to receive timestamp from Punit.
dccb.full_text: ula needs global art value over serial download wire from punit in gnr, ula had an integrated dvp (which is a recipient of global timestamp via adl from punit), thus providing access to the global timestamp.  in dmr, dvp is outside the ula, and ula needs time stamp for correlating its flit-logger with dvp.  hence, ula needs to implement a time sync serial download interface, in order to receive timestamp from punit.

dccb.title: [DMR-CCB][DMR.Power] [PnP Recovery] Update aventine phy L1 PS for MIO stack configured in UXI mode
dccb.description: Aventinue PHY supports various L1 power states that SOC can use depending up on its L1 exit tolerance. By default all MIOs use PS2 to balance power savings with exit latency of 10uS.  UXI enter L1 only during pkgC. Ask is to use PS4 for MIOs configured as UXI as it provides 150mW of power savings compared to PS2. Using PS4 increases L1 exit latency to 16uS. Higher L1 exit latency can be easily hidden behind other pkgC exit flows.  To achieve this BIOS needs to program following FBLP register for MIOs configured in UXI mode.

dccb.title: [DMR-CCB] Revision ID for DMR PCIe functions

dccb.title: [DMR-CCB] DMR FIVR Requires Synchronous Triggering
dccb.description: This circuit (source GNR/SRF:  22011493235 - [GNR] 7nm FIVR triggering circuit (intel.com) ) needs to be implemented in DMR IMH for FIVR triggering. From SOC distribution point, it is a star connection to all FIVRs. This is used for characterization of the FIVRs in post silicon, and must have all FIVRs see the trigger on the same clock cycle.  DMR and GNR have moved to an asynchronous trigger and there is no usync so that is being generated by the clock divider logic.  This implementation requires the clock init sequence to work.

dccb.title: [DMR-CCB] PrimeCode needs to program SCF credits for performance isolation mode
dccb.description: Default SCF credit programming done by PrimeCode is not sufficient for Perf Isolation Mode.  Each (B2D2D_CBB,HAMVF) pair needs to account for 2X the BW than the default assumption.  No buffer changes in either HAMVF or the B2D2D_CBB are expected, however, the credits do need to be allocated to each source differently than the default. To facilitate the mode-based programming of SCF credits, BIOS needs to inform PrimeCode via a CPU SoftStrap whether perf isolation mode is enabled or disabled.
dccb.full_text: [dmr-ccb] primecode needs to program scf credits for performance isolation mode default scf credit programming done by primecode is not sufficient for perf isolation mode.  each (b2d2d_cbb,hamvf) pair needs to account for 2x the bw than the default assumption.  no buffer changes in either hamvf or the b2d2d_cbb are expected, however, the credits do need to be allocated to each source differently than the default. to facilitate the mode-based programming of scf credits, bios needs to inform primecode via a cpu softstrap whether perf isolation mode is enabled or disabled.

dccb.title: [DMR-CCB][DMR-IMH2] Functional implications of CBB-IMH package routing change
dccb.description: DMR-AP (with IMH2) and DMR-SP (with reuse of IMH2) is changing the CBB-IMH2 connectivity over CBB-IMH1 connectivity to reduce cost by reducing IMH2 die height. This issue is to investigate any mainband or sideband implications of changing this connectivity to make sure all end-to-end flows involving CBB-IMH are not impacted, e.g., single DIMM config, sideband flows, etc.

dccb.title: [DMR-CCB] add support for new opcode to enable DDIO on CXL $
dccb.description: This is related to  https://hsdes.intel.com/appstore/article-one/#/article/22020316269 See thread below It looks like Citadel will not have the DDIO flows they need. They don’t use ItoMWr opcodes as the CXL DDIO feature suggests, instead they said they do RdOwnNoData (SpecItoM) and DirtyEvict (WbMtoI).   I checked with Dan, and there is no code in the CXL controller to map NT bit from the CXL link to the CacheFar bit on UFI for SpecItoM or DirtyEvict, so DDIO flows will never get triggered for these flows. My understanding is that if SCA never receives CacheFar=1, it will not trigger
dccb.full_text: [dmr-ccb] add support for new opcode to enable ddio on cxl $ this is related to  https://hsdes.intel.com/appstore/article-one/#/article/22020316269 see thread below it looks like citadel will not have the ddio flows they need. they don’t use itomwr opcodes as the cxl ddio feature suggests, instead they said they do rdownnodata (specitom) and dirtyevict (wbmtoi).   i checked with dan, and there is no code in the cxl controller to map nt bit from the cxl link to the cachefar bit on ufi for specitom or dirtyevict, so ddio flows will never get triggered for these flows. my understanding is that if sca never receives cachefar=1, it will not trigger

dccb.title: [DMR-CCB][DMR.Power] Remove MIO Power Gates and have Dedicated FIVR per stack
dccb.description: urrent POR-1 (f 16016254678  ) direction for MIO stack is to have per stack power gate. There are 2 FIVR to serve 3 MIO stack (1 FIVR for 2 MIO+x4 PCIe Gen4 and 1 FIVR for 3rd MIO).  New proposal under this feature is to remove power gates for MIO and have dedicated FIVR for each MIO stack (means add one more FIVR). Data shows it gives significant area benefit for area starwing MIO region  and gives power benefits across scenarios w.r.t. current POR-1.

dccb.title: [RDT] Support mixed Memory Regions for RDT
dccb.description: Move RDT to "regions" -- fix support for mixed memory regions, support 3-4 regions The current scheme, where we provide technology specific (i.e. DDR, 3DXP, CXL) QoS mechanisms doesn't match how our customers (and software) expect to use our machines. It doesn't comprehend e.g. interleaved regions of mixed DDR and CXL memory. It does not support systems with separate 1LM, 2LM and 3DXP tiers (or really any 3 tier configuration).
dccb.full_text: [rdt] support mixed memory regions for rdt move rdt to "regions" -- fix support for mixed memory regions, support 3-4 regions the current scheme, where we provide technology specific (i.e. ddr, 3dxp, cxl) qos mechanisms doesn't match how our customers (and software) expect to use our machines. it doesn't comprehend e.g. interleaved regions of mixed ddr and cxl memory. it does not support systems with separate 1lm, 2lm and 3dxp tiers (or really any 3 tier configuration).

(multual telemetry), trusted telemetry support LTM SRAM is likely not to in

dccb.title: [dmrhub-a0][DMR-CCB] IOCA Cache flush from Pcode
dccb.description: Current plan is to have 'only' allow Ucode to issue WBINVD to IOCA Cache in iMH. Recommendation is to let Pcode issue the GPSB CRRDs to Ubox register to initiate the IOCA WBINVD flush, to get the Token  ( store the 8b Token value internally) After the IOCA is flushed,  Ubox aggregates all rsps from ioca and update flush_status (token value) Pcode waits for 10s of us here and then start polling Pcode polls on it(for 16 instances) to get the indication, compares the 8b Flush status value with Token value, when its greater, that means flush has been completed.   iMh0.Pcode does iMH

dccb.title: [dmrhub-a0][DMR-CCB][ADR]Ensure Inflight txns from IOCA to LLC dont end up in Data pollution
dccb.description: Problem statement :With the recent inspection of the ADR flow in DMR IO Arch WG, we have found a bug with the addition of IOCA Cache in iMH where when all data traffic needs to be halted to LLC, IOCA might be starting traffic to LLC even after ph 1a. Assumption is that only after all the traffic has been halted to LLC and IOCA cache, we can start flushing both the caches in Ph1b. Right now, if there are inflight txns from HA asked from IOCA to CBO, there could be data pollution. Solution : Separation of IMH.IOCA flush and CBB.LLC flush i.e. IMH.IOCA flush will happen before CBB.LLC flush - IMH
dccb.full_text: [dmrhub-a0][dmr-ccb][adr]ensure inflight txns from ioca to llc dont end up in data pollution problem statement :with the recent inspection of the adr flow in dmr io arch wg, we have found a bug with the addition of ioca cache in imh where when all data traffic needs to be halted to llc, ioca might be starting traffic to llc even after ph 1a. assumption is that only after all the traffic has been halted to llc and ioca cache, we can start flushing both the caches in ph1b. right now, if there are inflight txns from ha asked from ioca to cbo, there could be data pollution. solution : separation of imh.ioca flush and cbb.llc flush i.e. imh.ioca flush will happen before cbb.llc flush - imh

dccb.title: [DMR][Change Request-Enhancement] HPM ADR message reformat
dccb.description: There will be a bit re-mapping going from GNR to DMR in HPM ADR message that is sent from Pri.Primecode to CBB.Pcode and to CXL devices, this is to align with the CXL spec in GPFType and GPF status fields Change description WE want to align the internal and CXL messages to be as close as possible. For this we move the SUB_PHASE to its own field in the internal message. GPFTYPE for CXL carries a bit to indicate if the caches should be flushed. WE want to use this internally to inform the leaf it should flush its cache. Root Primecode will set this bit based on the input from BIOS_SPARE register
dccb.full_text: [dmr][change request-enhancement] hpm adr message reformat there will be a bit re-mapping going from gnr to dmr in hpm adr message that is sent from pri.primecode to cbb.pcode and to cxl devices, this is to align with the cxl spec in gpftype and gpf status fields change description we want to align the internal and cxl messages to be as close as possible. for this we move the sub_phase to its own field in the internal message. gpftype for cxl carries a bit to indicate if the caches should be flushed. we want to use this internally to inform the leaf it should flush its cache. root primecode will set this bit based on the input from bios_spare register
